title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Dating app Hinge is expanding its One More Hour social impact initiative with a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London. The program will underwrite free or affordable in-person social events aimed at helping Gen Z, a group described as increasingly struggling with loneliness.
bullets: Hinge announced a $1 million fund for social groups.
Cities targeted: New York, Los Angeles, and London.
Funds are for free or affordable in-person social events for young people.
This is a global expansion of Hinge’s One More Hour initiative.
Context: Gen Z is described as increasingly experiencing loneliness.
takeaways: Concentration of funded events in NY, LA and London could mean localized, predictable increases in mobile and broadband demand during event times—monitor capacity in those metros.
Event-driven user activity may create short-duration spikes; ensure edge and last-mile resilience to maintain service quality for event attendees.
Partnerships or sponsorships from consumer platforms can drive recurring city-specific traffic patterns—track platform-driven initiatives as potential demand signals.
Visibility into event schedules from major apps can help ops teams plan maintenance windows and capacity scaling in targeted urban areas.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The session demonstrates building a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents and connecting it to an AI agent so the agent can understand a company’s business. It’s presented as an end-to-end demo that requires no graph expertise.
bullets: AI agents can perform general tasks but lack company-specific knowledge.
Knowledge graphs address that gap by encoding organizational knowledge.
The talk shows an automated pipeline: raw documents → knowledge graph → connected agent.
No prior graph theory or query expertise is required.
Presented as an end-to-end build; references a prior episode comparing VectorDB vs GraphDB.
takeaways: Knowledge-graph-powered agents make company-specific AI interactions feasible—expect interest in deploying such solutions.
Automated graph construction reduces reliance on specialized graph expertise, shifting focus to operational support.
Plan for infrastructure to handle document ingestion, graph storage, and agent integration as these workflows are demonstrated end-to-end.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum of PwC US stresses that energy tax credits are only part of the sustainability tax picture and that businesses should also consider less obvious incentives. She highlights state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation (for example, reimagining packaging or processes), concluding that a broader view of tax incentives can unlock meaningful tax value from sustainability investments.
bullets: Energy incentives are commonly top of mind but aren’t the whole story.
State and local abatements can support sustainability initiatives.
Location-based incentives may be available depending on site choices.
R&D tax credits can apply to sustainability-related innovation like reimagining packaging or processes.
Viewing sustainability investments through a broader tax lens can unlock meaningful tax value.
takeaways: Don’t assume only energy credits apply—assess state and local abatements when planning grid/data center projects.
Consider location-based incentives as part of site selection and expansion decisions.
Evaluate whether R&D tax credits could apply to operational or design innovations in data center processes.
Treat sustainability investments as potential sources of tax value by looking beyond the obvious incentives.

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the short video frames Copilot as helping Christa "find the star within," using a sky-full-of-stars metaphor. It closes with a call to action to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: Presents Copilot guiding Christa to discover her "inner star."
Uses sky and stars imagery as the central metaphor.
Promotional, encouraging viewers to try Copilot.
Call to action: download via link in bio.
No transcript is available.
takeaways: The video markets Copilot as a consumer-facing, downloadable experience (link in bio), so anticipate user access/download traffic patterns.
Promotional emphasis on personal empowerment suggests consumer interest in AI assistants—plan for scalable, user-focused service delivery.
Simple, shareable short-form content can drive downloads; ensure distribution channels and update mechanisms are reliable.

items.title: Bringing In $251K A Year With 3 Jobs In The Bay Area

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Naseema McElroy is a 44-year-old labor and delivery nurse in Brentwood, California who also runs a personal finance brand called Financially Intentional. She accumulated nearly $1 million in debt in 2015, documented her debt-payoff journey, became debt-free by 2017, and now brings in $251K a year working three jobs in the Bay Area.
bullets: Nurse and founder of Financially Intentional documenting her debt-payoff journey.
Accumulated nearly $1 million in debt in 2015; debt-free by 2017.
Currently earns $251,000 annually by holding three jobs in the Bay Area.
Started the business to share lessons from her financial turnaround.
Profiled as part of CNBC Make It’s Millennial Money series.
takeaways: High regional costs can lead employees to pursue multiple income sources—compensation competitiveness matters for retention.
Financially stressed workers may turn to entrepreneurship or side gigs; employers could consider financial-wellness resources.
Scheduling flexibility can be important for staff who balance multiple jobs or external commitments.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, NVIDIA's GeForce Garage video spotlights the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc community contest, u/JeffersonPutnam, who built a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The build pairs a Fractal Design Era 2 chassis and MSI MPG B850I Edge mini‑ITX board with a Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, a Corsair SF1000 PSU and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam’s SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build (Summer of RTX community contest).
Key components: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, MSI MPG B850I Edge (mini‑ITX).
Chassis and cooling: Fractal Design Era 2 with Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
Power: Corsair SF1000 SFX PSU to support the high‑end GPU in a compact case.
Video host: GeForce host Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato); full parts list on PCPartPicker.
takeaways: High‑end GPUs like the RTX 5090 can be integrated into small‑form‑factor builds, but require attention to power delivery (SFX 1000W) and cooling solutions.
Mini‑ITX motherboards (MSI MPG B850I Edge) enable compact systems while pairing with top‑tier CPUs (Ryzen 7 9800X3D) for balanced compute performance.
Chassis choice (Fractal Era 2) and an AIO cooler (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) are crucial for managing thermal density in compact enclosures.
Selecting appropriately rated SFX power supplies is important when deploying high‑power GPUs in space‑constrained designs.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description… TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy GenAI solutions on Google Cloud, accelerating its digital transformation and unlocking new revenue streams. The engagement focused on leveraging Google Cloud's infrastructure to streamline operations and boost developer productivity while compressing the transformation timeline.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were central to the solution.
The partnership aimed to streamline operations and improve developer productivity.
Efforts compressed TTEC’s digital transformation timeline.
New revenue streams were unlocked through deployed AI capabilities.
takeaways: Investing in cloud infrastructure and GenAI can shorten transformation timelines and speed time-to-value.
Partnering with cloud consulting can accelerate prototyping and deployment of AI solutions.
Improvements in developer productivity can directly support faster rollouts and operational efficiency.
AI-enabled services can create new revenue opportunities when combined with robust cloud platforms.
